title: Tephroseris aurea
intro: Tephroseris aurea, also known as Golden Horse Tail, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Golden Horse Tail include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Golden Horse Tail include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Tephroseris aurea is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or aster family. It is a part of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants that are often referred to as ragworts or hawkweeds. Tephroseris aurea is a genus of flowering plants, which is a classification level below the family. The classification of Tephroseris aurea is further refined to the level of species, where it is given its full scientific name. Understanding the classification of Tephroseris aurea is essential for its identification and study in the field of botany.
